reference.title: Before and After: Complete Transformation Examples
reference.shortdesc: Side-by-side comparison of real training content showing the complete transformation from unstructured XML to AI-ready DITA, with annotations explaining why each change matters for chatbot quality.

section.title: Example 1: Concept topic — Explaining content reuse
section.codeblock:
DITA Reuse and Variants

Overview

Content reuse is a key benefit of DITA. You can reuse content at multiple levels using different mechanisms.

Conref

The conref mechanism lets you pull content from one topic into another. It works like a symlink for content.

Example:

<p conref="shared/warnings.dita#warnings/safety-warning"/>

Keyref

Keys provide indirect references. Define a key in your map, then reference it in topics.

DITAVAL Filtering

Use DITAVAL files to include or exclude content based on audience, platform, or product attributes.

Best Practices

  • Always use keys for product names
  • Prefer keyref over conref when possible
  • Test all variants before publishing

Reuse, Keys, and Variant Management How to write content once and assemble it into multiple deliverables using conref, keyref, conkeyref, and DITAVAL filtering. Extense LLC Content reuse Conref Keyref DITAVAL Conditional processing taxonomy:content-reuse technical-writer developer
Why does content reuse matter?

Reuse eliminates duplication...

How does conref work?

The conref mechanism pulls content from one topic into another at publish time...

<p conref="shared/warnings.dita#warnings/safety"/>
How do keys and keyref work?

Keys provide indirect references...

How does DITAVAL filtering work?

DITAVAL files include or exclude content...

What are the best practices for reuse?
  • Always use keys for product names
  • Prefer keyref over conref
  • Test all variants before publishing

row.entry: This content explains ideas, not procedures
AI formats answer as explanation, not steps
Serves as chunk summary in vector search
Every chunk inherits this summary for context
Users search by multiple terms for the same concept
Both "DITAVAL" and "conditional processing" queries find this topic
Domain-filtered retrieval: only return reuse content when user asks about reuse
Declares this explains a concept, not instructs a task
"What is conref?" routes here; "How do I set up conref?" routes to a task topic
Matches how users ask the chatbot
"How does conref work?" query matches section title directly, boosting retrieval score
Chatbot can suggest "Read DITA Authoring first" to beginners

section.title: Example 2: Task topic — Installing DITA-OT
section.codeblock:
Installing DITA-OT

You need Java 17+ installed first.

  1. Go to https://www.dita-ot.org/download
  2. Download the latest .zip release
  3. Unzip to a directory (e.g., ~/dita-ot)
  4. Add ~/dita-ot/bin to your PATH
  5. Test: run dita --version

If you see the version number, you're all set!

Installing DITA-OT Download, install, and verify DITA Open Toolkit 4.x on your machine.
  • Java 17 or later — Check with java -version.
  • 500 MB free disk space
  • Network access to download
 Download the latest release from dita-ot.org/download Choose the .zip distribution for your platform. Unzip to your preferred directory. unzip dita-ot-4.2.zip -d ~/ A dita-ot-4.2/ directory is created. Add the bin/ directory to your PATH. export PATH=~/dita-ot-4.2/bin:$PATH

Run dita --version. You should see: DITA-OT version 4.2.

ul.li: Prerequisites are semantically separated — AI can show them before the steps, and warn users if prerequisites are not met
Steps use <cmd> — the imperative instruction is clearly separated from supporting info, examples, and results
<stepresult> — AI can tell users what to expect after each step
<result> — AI can describe the expected outcome to help users verify success
content-intent="how-to" — "How do I install DITA-OT?" queries are routed directly here

section.title: Example 3: The metadata difference — same topic, two quality levels
section.p: This example shows the same DITA topic at two metadata quality levels, demonstrating the before/after of the metadata enrichment process:
section.codeblock: <concept id="docker-basics"> <title>Docker for DITA-OT</title> <shortdesc>Using the official DITA-OT Docker image for consistent builds.</shortdesc> <prolog> <author>Extense LLC</author> <metadata> <keywords> <indexterm>Docker</indexterm> <indexterm>Container</indexterm> </keywords> </metadata> </prolog> ... </concept>
section.p: What the chatbot can do: Find this topic via keyword match ("Docker") or vector similarity. That's it. No filtering by audience, difficulty, domain, or intent.
section.codeblock: <concept id="docker-basics" xml:lang="en-US"> <title>Docker for DITA-OT: Local Builds and Reproducible Toolchains</title> <shortdesc>Using the official DITA-OT Docker image for consistent, reproducible builds that eliminate environment differences across developer machines and CI.</shortdesc> <prolog> <author>Extense LLC</author> <critdates> <created date="2026-02-10"/> </critdates> <metadata> <keywords> <indexterm>Docker</indexterm> <indexterm>Container</indexterm> <indexterm>Reproducible builds</indexterm> </keywords> <category>taxonomy:devops</category> <audience>developer</audience> <audience>build-engineer</audience> </metadata> <resourceid appid="extense-training" appname="chatbot" id="docker-basics"/> <data name="difficulty" value="intermediate"/> <data name="duration" value="PT20M"/> <data name="content-domain" value="devops"/> <data name="content-intent" value="explain"/> <data name="chatbot-priority" value="high"/> <data name="service-line" value="publishing-engineering"/> <data name="prerequisites" value="dita-publishing"/> </prolog> ... </concept>

p.b: What the chatbot can now do:

ul.li: Filter for audience=developer — show this only to developers, not content strategists
Filter for difficulty=intermediate — don't show to absolute beginners
Route content-domain=devops — only return when user asks about devops topics
Match content-intent=explain — show for "what is..." questions, not "how to..." questions
Apply chatbot-priority=high — rank above low-priority reference material
Suggest prerequisites=dita-publishing — "You should read DITA Publishing first"
Use critdates — rank fresher content higher

section.title: Summary: What changes at each transformation stage

row.entry: Vector + keyword + metadata boost + priority weighting
